steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects 'a broad pattern... for which negative words saw a faster rate of cognate replacement' with 'the rate at which a word will be replaced over time with a noncognate form.' The second phrase is a plain-language definition of 'cognate replacement rate' — the same idea, restated more simply. The transition must signal a restatement or clarification.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — likewise,: signals similarity between two different ideas, not a restatement of the same idea — ✗
  • Choice B — that is,: means 'in other words' — exactly the signal for restating or clarifying a term just introduced — ✓
  • Choice C — for example,: introduces a specific instance of a general claim, but the sentence that follows is a definition, not an example — ✗
  • Choice D — in addition,: adds a new point; the second phrase adds no new information, it only clarifies — ✗
That matches Choice B — that is,.
